$MSFT and $AMZN closing green while $META holds flat into a weak overall tape isn’t something I’d brush off as random.

$MU reports tomorrow.

discussion_digest: The biggest story today is Micron (MU) earnings anxiety. MU is the most mentioned stock on Reddit with 1,278 mentions, up from 965 yesterday, and Stocktwits sentiment is bullish (17 vs 5). However, top messages are nervous: one trader doubled their position but said 'god save us lol,' while another warned that if MU misses, 'it's wraps for the AI bull run.' The community is on edge, with expectations sky-high for Q3 revenue and record margins. A beat could fuel the rally, but a miss could trigger a sector-wide selloff.

Other notable discussions include Cerebras (CBRS) post-IPO turmoil, with mentions exploding from 1 to 56 after its first earnings report caused a 9% after-hours drop. The Stocktwits community is split (12 bullish vs 14 bearish), with top messages highlighting insider share unlocks on June 25 and exclusivity issues with OpenAI. Meanwhile, IBM and DTE Energy are being discussed as safe havens, with IBM jumping from rank 80 to 8 on Reddit and having extremely bullish sentiment (18 vs 1). Alphabet's addition to the Dow is also a topic, but the reaction is muted.

The crowd disagrees sharply on MU and CBRS. For MU, some are doubling down expecting a beat, while others fear a miss could end the AI run. For CBRS, bulls see a buying opportunity at NVDA-like prices, while bears point to insider unlocks and a 'complete disaster.' The broader market sentiment is bearish, with SPY and QQQ having bearish tags (10 vs 25 and 9 vs 15) and warnings of a 'limit down day.' This tension suggests that investors are bracing for volatility, with defensive plays like IBM and DTE gaining favor.

overlooked_insights.title: CBRS Insider Unlock on June 25
overlooked_insights.insight: Exactly 24.05 million insider shares become eligible to hit the market on Thursday, June 25, 2026. This massive unlock could create significant selling pressure on top of the post-earnings decline, as insiders may look to cash out after the stock's tumble.
overlooked_insights.evidence: A user posted: 'No shares are unlocking tomorrow (Wednesday), but this Thursday, June 25, 2026, exactly 24.05 million insider shares will legally become eligible to hit the market.'
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Mainstream coverage focused on Cerebras' earnings miss and margin warning, not the imminent insider lockup expiration and its potential impact.

overlooked_insights.title: IBM Quantum Foundry Expansion and Options Activity
overlooked_insights.insight: IBM is scaling its quantum infrastructure with a new 'quantum foundry' in New York, reportedly moving toward 300mm quantum wafer fabrication. At the same time, heavy call options activity on the July 17 $300 strike suggests traders are betting on a near-term move higher.
overlooked_insights.evidence: A user wrote: 'IBM is pushing deeper into quantum infrastructure with a new “quantum foundry” expansion in New York... scaling toward a 300mm quantum wafer fabrication capability.' Another said: '300 c for July 17 loaded to the gills.'
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Headlines highlighted IBM's OpenAI cybersecurity deal and the broader tech rally, but omitted the specific manufacturing scale-up details and the concentrated options positioning.

overlooked_insights.title: META Internal $300B Capex Rumor and AI Safety Pressure
overlooked_insights.insight: Multiple users claim that Meta employees are buzzing about CEO Mark Zuckerberg raising capital expenditures to $300 billion soon, far above current levels. Separately, users report the Trump administration is pressing Meta to submit its AI models for government safety reviews, adding regulatory uncertainty.
overlooked_insights.evidence: A user stated: 'Rumors inside meta from employees' and another: 'Zuckerberg 300 billion Capex coming.' A separate message said: 'Meta is reportedly getting pressure from the Trump administration to put its AI models through voluntary government safety reviews.'
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Mainstream articles cover Meta's smart glasses and prediction markets, but ignore the explosive internal capex rumor and the specific regulatory pressure from the White House.

overlooked_insights.title: DTE-LG Energy Storage Partnership
overlooked_insights.insight: DTE Energy has entered a clean energy partnership with LG Energy Solution Vertech to deploy eight new energy storage projects across Michigan, with a combined investment of $1.6 billion. This represents a substantial growth catalyst for grid reliability and renewable integration.
overlooked_insights.evidence: A user posted: 'DTE Energy enters a clean energy partnership with LG Energy Solution Vertech... will roll out eight new energy storage projects... combined investment of $1.6 billion.'
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: The current headlines only mention management changes and dividend announcements, completely missing this large-scale energy storage deal.

overlooked_insights.title: MU Stock Split Hopes and $1.1M Options Bet
overlooked_insights.insight: Users express frustration that MU's share price near $1,000 excludes small investors, leading to speculation about a stock split. Separately, one user claims to have purchased 150 contracts of $1,100 strike calls, a $1.65 million notional bet. The earnings bar is set extremely high, with calls for record margins above 81%.
overlooked_insights.evidence: A user said: 'I sure hope they do a stock split tomorrow. Even at this price 10 measly shares would cost 10,000.' Another wrote: 'We are betting $1100 call options. 150 contracts.' A third: 'They have to beat the highest end of their guidance for Q3 revenue, record margins (81%+).'
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Mainstream coverage focuses on the overall tech sell-off and earnings preview, but overlooks the specific options activity and the retail-driven stock split narrative.

overlooked_insights.title: SNDK Drop Blamed on Leveraged ETFs
overlooked_insights.insight: A user argues that the sharp drop in Sandisk (and other memory stocks) was caused by retail investors piling into leveraged ETFs, triggering a mechanical unwind rather than a fundamental change in AI demand. This suggests the sell-off may be overdone and could reverse.
overlooked_insights.evidence: A user commented: 'it’s hard to believe individual investors buying leveraged ETFs crashed this thing.'
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: News attributes the decline to 'AI spending concerns' and sector rotation, without considering the role of leveraged ETF flows in amplifying the move.

overlooked_insights.title: FDX Drop Seen as Excessive Despite Beat
overlooked_insights.insight: Despite FedEx beating Q4 earnings and revenue estimates, the stock fell sharply due to weak FY2027 guidance. However, multiple users view the drop as overdone, noting the underlying business performance was solid and that the sell-off may present a buying opportunity.
overlooked_insights.evidence: Users posted: 'excessive drop. Looking at the numbers this should have went the other way' and 'great QTR' and 'relax guyz Tomorrow 315' (bullish price target).
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: Mainstream headlines emphasize the guidance miss as the reason for the decline, but the crowd sees a disconnect between the earnings beat and the magnitude of the sell-off.

overlooked_insights.title: SPY Specific Support Levels: $720-728
overlooked_insights.insight: Multiple users are calling for SPY to test support around $720-728 in the coming days, with one even predicting a 'limit down day.' These precise technical levels are being discussed as key downside targets that could define the near-term market direction.
overlooked_insights.evidence: Users stated: 'SPY 720’s', 'SPY $721 by Thursday', 'SPY will go down more $728 first', 'SPY $725 retest seems inevitable.'
overlooked_insights.why_overlooked: General market coverage talks about a 'sell-off' and 'AI concerns' but does not provide the specific support levels that the trading community is focused on.

crowd_vs_news.news_narrative: FedEx stock drops on weak FY2027 profit forecast despite a Q4 earnings beat.
crowd_vs_news.crowd_view: The drop is excessive; the underlying earnings were strong and the stock should have gone higher, making this a potential buying opportunity.
crowd_vs_news.who_is_right: The crowd has a point – the magnitude of the decline (-6% after-hours) seems harsh given the earnings beat, but the weak guidance does justify some caution. The truth likely lies in between.

crowd_vs_news.news_narrative: Sandisk falls as part of a broad chip sell-off driven by AI spending concerns and sector rotation.
crowd_vs_news.crowd_view: The drop is mechanically caused by retail investors piling into leveraged ETFs rather than any change in business fundamentals, implying the sell-off is overdone.
crowd_vs_news.who_is_right: The leveraged ETF theory is plausible but unproven; broad market forces are likely at play. The crowd may be underestimating the impact of AI spending concerns, but the mechanic of ETF flow could amplify moves.

crowd_vs_news.news_narrative: Cerebras stock falls 9% after-hours due to mixed Q1 earnings and a margin warning, with investors disappointed by 2026 sales outlook.
crowd_vs_news.crowd_view: The real story is the upcoming insider unlock (24 million shares) and the exclusivity deal with OpenAI that limits tenant diversification, not just the earnings miss.
crowd_vs_news.who_is_right: The crowd correctly identifies a critical near-term catalyst (the unlock) that the headlines missed. However, the earnings disappointment is also a valid concern. Both angles matter.

MU.summary: Micron (MU) is the center of attention as it prepares to report earnings. The community is extremely polarized: bulls are betting on a blowout quarter driven by AI demand for high-bandwidth memory (HBM), while bears warn that expectations are so high that even a slight miss could tank the stock and potentially end the AI rally. Many traders are loading up on call options, expecting a big move. However, there's also fear that the stock could get 'wiped' like Cerebras (a competitor) and that retail investors might get trapped. Overall, the sentiment is a mix of extreme optimism and caution, with the earnings report being the key catalyst. Investors should know that MU is a high-beta play on AI infrastructure, and the stock could swing wildly based on the earnings outcome.

META.summary: The online chatter around META is a mixed bag of frustration, hope, and skepticism. On Reddit, mentions jumped from 78 to 91 in 24 hours, ranking 9th overall, indicating heightened interest. Stocktwits shows a bullish tilt with 9 bullish tags vs 5 bearish. The messages reveal a split: some users are furious about the stock's price action after earnings—complaining that good news leads to drops while bad news amplifies declines. Others are bullish on Meta's smart glasses and massive user base, predicting a run to $800 by summer. A recurring negative theme is Mark Zuckerberg's massive capex plans (rumored $300 billion), with critics calling him a spendthrift who will destroy value. Overall, the community is polarized: bears see reckless spending and poor market reaction, while bulls bet on the glasses ecosystem and user monetization. Investors should note the high volatility and conflicting narratives—earnings beats haven't translated to price gains, and capex fears loom large. The sentiment is cautious but with a vocal bullish minority.

FDX.summary: FedEx is getting a lot of attention after its recent earnings report, but the buzz is mostly negative. The company beat earnings expectations for the quarter, but its guidance for fiscal 2027 was way below what Wall Street expected, causing the stock to drop over 5% in after-hours trading. People on Stocktwits are frustrated, with some calling for the stock to go to zero and others noting the drop seems excessive given the numbers. There's a sense that this is a bad omen for the broader market, as FedEx is often seen as a bellwether for the economy. Some traders are looking at puts or expecting further declines, with one user predicting $275-280 by Friday. However, there are a few bullish voices, like one user suggesting a call option play with a 56% potential upside. The overall sentiment is bearish, with the weak guidance overshadowing the earnings beat. Investors should know that FedEx is in a transition year after spinning off its freight division, and management's cautious outlook reflects ongoing challenges in the shipping industry, including softer demand and cost pressures. The stock's drop might present a buying opportunity for contrarians, but the near-term outlook is uncertain.
